title: Roadmap (brand-wide instance)
description: Roadmap = matrice production brand-wide. Un seul roadmap par brand (cohérent avec Notion stride-up 'Roadmap [angles/audiences]' par workspace, pas par axe). Stored at brands/{slug}/roadmap.json (singleton, distinct du pattern par-instance angles/frictions). Closes the Notion stride-up parity gap (Roadmap 50% coverage pre-v2.56). Cross-ref doctrine : compositional-cartography.md (roadmap = matrice production des outputs créatifs, axe orthogonal au substrate canon ; alimente la planification de NOYAU × CONTEXTE × MODIFIEURS dans le temps). phases[] capture l'horizon temporel (sprints, cycles), mix[] capture la pondération stratégique par axe (audience / angle / product / funnel / creative), production_status[] capture l'état concret de chaque livrable. relations agrège les ids cross-référencés pour query rapide. v1.0 baseline shippée v2.56.

_changelog.1.0: v2.56 initial release · Notion stride-up parity Roadmap angles/audiences. Closes le gap où aucun schema canon ne capturait la matrice production brand-wide (planning par phase, pondération mix, statut par livrable). v1.0 fields : roadmap_id (RDM-{brand_slug}), meta (validation_status ref _shared + _source enum), phases[] (timeline avec status enum), mix[] (pondération weight 0-1 par axis enum 5 valeurs), production_status[] (état par livrable cross-entity), relations (agrégat ids angle/audience/product/creative). Storage singleton brands/{slug}/roadmap.json (distinct pattern par-instance des autres schemas, justifié par modèle Notion stride-up 'un roadmap par workspace'). Backward compat : N/A (nouveau schema). Source canon : Notion stride-up gap analysis v2.56.

_schema_version.description: Backward compat alias of root `_schema_version`. New readers SHOULD read the root field.

roadmap_id.description: RDM-{brand_slug} format. One roadmap per brand, id mirrors the brand slug for unambiguous resolution. E.g. 'RDM-stepprs', 'RDM-we-bet'. Pattern accepts lowercase, digits, dashes (matches brand slug convention).

phases.description: Temporal segmentation of the roadmap. Each phase = one planning horizon (sprint, quarter, season). Order matters (chronological). Phases can overlap if explicitly modeled by dates.

phase_id.description: PHS-NN format. Stable, never reassigned. Used as foreign key from production_status[].phase_ref (if added in future versions).

name.description: Operator-facing label. E.g. 'Q3 2026 Launch', 'Sprint 14 Retention', 'Saison automne'.

dates.description: Phase boundaries. Both optional (open-ended phase = ongoing, no end).

status.description: Phase-level status (aggregate). Backlog = identified, not committed. Committed = scoped + scheduled. In production = work actively underway. Live = active in-market. Done = completed and archived. Cancelled = abandoned (kept for historical record).

priorities.description: Free-form priority labels for this phase. E.g. ['retention', 'wedge-anti-tampering', 'cohort-Q3-2025']. Operator-defined, no enum (taxonomy emerges from usage).

mix.description: Strategic weighting per axis. Captures the pondération across audience / angle / product / funnel / creative. Sum of weights per axis SHOULD approach 1.0 for normalized mix, but not enforced (operator may model partial mix). Used by produce-paid-angles, score-matrix to bias output toward declared priorities.

weight.description: Strategic weight 0.0-1.0. 0 = deprioritized. 1 = exclusive focus. Mid values = relative emphasis. Operator-stated or skill-derived from past performance.

axis.description: Which dimension this mix entry weighs. audience = profile slug emphasis. angle = ANG-NN emphasis. product = product slug emphasis. funnel = funnel stage (TOF/MOF/BOF) emphasis. creative = creative typology emphasis.

target_id.description: Optional reference to the specific entity weighted. E.g. axis=audience + target_id='dad-protective-of-daughters'. Null = axis-level weighting (e.g. 'angles weight 0.4 globally vs audiences weight 0.6').

rationale.description: Operator notes on why this weighting. Free-form.

production_status.description: Concrete production state per deliverable. One entry per entity in production. Used to surface 'what's live / what's stuck / what's coming' at a glance. Operator-facing dashboard renders this directly.

entity_type.description: Which canon entity this production line tracks. Matches the schema names (angle.schema.json, profile.schema.json, spec.schema.json, creative.schema.json, funnel.schema.json).

entity_id.description: Id of the tracked entity. ANG-NN for angle, audience slug for audience, product slug for product, CRE-NN for creative, funnel id for funnel. Pattern not enforced here (delegated to referenced schema).

target_date.description: Committed delivery date. Null = no commitment yet.

status.description: Production lifecycle. Backlog = identified, not started. Committed = scheduled, brief locked. In production = being built. Live = shipped, running. Done = archived (deprecated, replaced, or saturated). Cancelled = abandoned.

phase_ref.description: Optional reference to a phase[].phase_id. Ties this deliverable to a planning horizon.

owner.description: Operator or team member accountable. Free-form.

blockers.description: Active blockers (asset missing, validation pending, dependency on other entity). Free-form strings.

relations.description: Aggregated cross-references across the roadmap. Enables fast lookup ('what entities does this roadmap touch ?') without scanning production_status[]. Auto-derivable from production_status + mix but kept as denormalized view for read performance.

angle_ids.description: All angle ids referenced anywhere in the roadmap.

audience_slugs.description: All audience slugs referenced anywhere in the roadmap.

product_slugs.description: All product slugs referenced anywhere in the roadmap.

creative_ids.description: All creative ids referenced anywhere in the roadmap.

validation_status.description: Lifecycle of the roadmap-as-encoded-entity. hypothesis = draft plan, not committed. tested = phase 1 underway. validated = phase 1 shipped + measured. scaled = roadmap actively driving production rhythm. fatigued = obsolete, awaiting refresh.

_source.description: Where the roadmap came from. observed = derived from actual production cadence (campaigns shipped, briefs produced). inferred = agent-deduced from brand stage + strategy.json. declared = operator stated explicitly (planning session, intake interview). Auto-tagged from semantic signal; never exposed to operator with this literal label (operator sees 'observé / déduit / déclaré' translation per HR-20).

created_by_skill.description: Which skill generated this roadmap. E.g. 'plan-roadmap', 'snapshot-brand', 'manual' (operator-entered via Notion-like UI).

horizon.description: Planning rhythm. Operator preference. Influences how phases[] are typically sized.

_field_types.description: Per-field type tag (observed | stated | derived | structured). Drives mutation guard. Reference: docs/system/field-types.md.
